summary: Early documented ancestor in the Amir Nezam Letgahi chain.
Son of Haji Mohammad Khan Jeyhunabadi in the published lineage.

Hajilu commander known as E'temad al-Saltaneh. He commanded the Fadavi military establishment, served in the southern war against Britain, held command in Astarabad and Khorasan, and was father of Abdollah Khan Sa'ed al-Saltaneh.

Leading figure of the Amir Nezam family of Latgah. Son of Mostafa-Qoli Khan E'temad al-Saltaneh; father of Hossein-Qoli, Mohtaj-Ali, and Mansur-Ali. Governor of Khuzestan and major landholder associated with Latgah and Kabudarahang.

Son of Abdollah Khan Sardar Akram and member of the Amir Nezam family. Confirmed titles: Ejlal al-Mamalek and Amir Arfa'. The book gives little independent career detail beyond lineage, titles, and family-house context.
Son of Abdollah Khan; titled Sardar Akram. No descendants are shown in the book's figure.
Probable son of Mohtaj-Ali Khan and brother of Gholamhossein Khan according to family testimony and the difficult genealogy diagram. S0001 provides no substantial independent narrative biography.

Paternal grandfather of the archive owner. Son of Mohtaj-Ali Khan and brother of Gholam-Ali according to family testimony; the small genealogy diagram is only supporting evidence. S0001 does not provide a securely identified narrative biography.
Father of Hossein Gholi Amiri Gharagozloo; born in Hamadan.
Project owner and family historian.

Early Ashiqloo chief and major Zand/early-Qajar commander. The narrative explicitly names five sons: Rostam, Abdollah Sarem al-Saltaneh, Nabi, Fazlollah, and Ali-Naqi. Shahbaz Khan is treated as his probable father or predecessor, not a proven parent.
Son of Abdollah Khan; Ejlal al-Dowleh and Amir Tuman; later titled Sa'ed al-Saltaneh.
Son of Mohammad Hossein Khan; senior Gharagozloo commander killed after being severely wounded in the Herat campaign.
Mirza Mahmud Khan Naser al-Molk Gharagozloo; grandfather and predecessor of Abu al-Qasem Khan Naser al-Molk.
Yavar who supported Nabi Khan’s forces at Herat, was wounded, and received the rank of sarhang. Identity is not yet merged with other Abdollah Khans.
Gharagozloo soldier who repeatedly climbed the Herat fortress wall and attempted to plant the victory flag.
Colonel of the Mokhberan regiment wounded in the leg during the Herat fighting.
Gharagozloo brigadier active in the southern campaigns against British forces.
Son of Rustam Khan; senior Gharagozloo officer active in southern Iran and Kerman.
Commander of the Dargazin infantry regiment in the Laristan campaign.
Commander associated with the sixth Gharagozloo regiment in Kerman.

preferred_name_en: Mohammad Hossein Khan Hosam al-Molk Gharagozloo
summary: Son of Ali Khan; Gharagozloo officer rewarded for service in Kerman.

preferred_name_en: Zeyn al-Abedin Khan Hosam al-Molk Gharagozloo
summary: Prominent Gharagozloo khan active in Kermanshah and Hamadan politics.
Son of Zeyn al-Abedin Khan; married Qamar al-Saltaneh in 1309 AH.
Gharagozloo officer with the rank of yavar, involved in a diplomatic incident with the Russian embassy in 1313 AH.
Servant of Aqa Jan Gharagozloo involved in the death of a Russian coachman.
Russian coachman killed during an altercation involving Hasan Tupchi.
Son of Mohammad Hossein Khan; husband of a daughter of Mohammad Shah and father of Negar Khanom.
Daughter of Abdollah Khan Sarem al-Saltaneh; wife of Ahmad Khan Sartip and later Abd al-Samad Mirza.
Son of Mahmoud Khan Naser al-Molk; father of Abu al-Qasem Khan Naser al-Molk.
Son of Nabi Khan; founder of the Baha al-Molk Abshini family.

preferred_name_en: Hossein Qoli Khan Zia al-Molk Gharagozloo
summary: Son of Amanollah Khan and progenitor of the Zia al-Molk Hamadani line.

preferred_name_en: Ali Qoli Khan Baha al-Molk Gharagozloo
summary: Son of Amanollah Khan and progenitor of the Baha al-Molk Hamadani line.
Son of Amanollah Khan and progenitor of the Sangestani line.
Constitutionalist preacher killed in Borujerd by order of Amir Afkham.
Daughter of Mozaffar al-Din Shah and wife of Gholamreza Khan Ehtesham al-Dowleh.
Educated Gharagozloo notable elected from Hamadan to the Third Majles and later finance minister.
Son of Mehdi Khan Amir Tuman; third secretary at the Iranian legation in London, 1299-1303 SH.
Representative of Hamadan in the Constituent Assembly of 1304 SH.

preferred_name_en: Mirza Abu al-Qasem Khan Naser al-Molk Gharagozloo
summary: Ashiqloo statesman, son of Ahmad Khan Sartip and grandson of Mahmoud Khan Naser al-Molk. Educated at Oxford, court translator, diplomat, finance minister, prime minister, and later regent during Ahmad Shah's minority.
Son of Naser al-Molk; associated with Othello translation preface; married Iran Khanom, daughter of Teymurtash
Son of Naser al-Molk; criticized in later memoir literature for dissipation
Chapter states Naser al-Molk had two sons and one daughter
Father-in-law and chief overseer of Naser al-Molk estates
Estate manager
Son of Mirza Mohammad Khan; estate manager
Appointed Naser al-Molk prime minister; later ordered his arrest/possible execution
Minor king during Naser al-Molk's regency
Predecessor as regent; died 17 Ramadan 1328 AH
Closed the Second Majles on Naser al-Molk's order
American financial adviser dismissed after Russian ultimatum
Criticized closure of Second Majles
Intervened to save Naser al-Molk from Mohammad Ali Shah
Father of Iran Khanom, wife of Hossein Ali Gharagozloo
Married Hossein Ali Gharagozloo; later divorced
Member of Naser al-Molk cabinet: Minister of Justice
Member of Naser al-Molk cabinet: Minister of Foreign Affairs
Member of Naser al-Molk cabinet: Minister of Interior
Member of Naser al-Molk cabinet: Minister of War
Member of Naser al-Molk cabinet: Minister of Education and Public Works
Member of Naser al-Molk cabinet: Minister of Commerce
Named as colonel of the Sixth Gharagozloo Regiment.
Named as colonel of the Mansur Regiment.
Named as son of Naser al-Molk and commander/colonel of the Mokhberan and Sixth regiments in different contexts.
Named as eldest son of Mehdi Khan and colonel of the Mokhberan Regiment.
Commander named in the late Seljuk narrative; associated with Sultan Tughril III and the conspiracy against Qizil Arslan.
Governor of Hamadan named in the struggles following the death of Qizil Arslan.
Veteran commander serving Mohammad-Zaman Mirza; killed while acting as an envoy in 925 AH. His connection to the later Gharagozloos is explicitly uncertain.
Named among the Qizilbash officers accompanying Humayun from Qandahar toward Kabul in 952 AH.
Safavid official who served as beglerbeg of Astarabad, Gilan, and Mazandaran and was appointed governor of Kerman in 1129 AH.
Leader named in a report of resistance to Afghan forces near Tabriz in 1138 AH.
Gharagozloo leader who joined Karim Khan Zand with a force described as two thousand men.
Influential Zand-era Gharagozloo commander and political actor.
Eldest son of Mohammad-Hossein Khan in the narrative; increasingly assumed command of Gharagozloo forces late in his father's life.
One of the five sons explicitly named for the early Mohammad-Hossein Khan; later military commander.
